floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: t/u round-trip is byte-real (target has same-slot lw/sw pairs) -> respell as setVector idiom; nested do-while(0) wrappers likely load-bearing sched fences (sanctionable w/ FAKE per

Root-caused the whole function: the do-while(0) wrappers are sched.c:2066 TOTAL scheduling barriers (flush_pending_lists + all-reg deps), NOT the sanctioned reorg.c do-while(0) mechanism -- so the rul

frontier.hypothesis: STATEMENT ORDER is the un-swept lever. The target's bytes pin BOTH (a) at-form per-symbol stores + per-copy lui/addiu bases = the codegen of separate scalar symbols, and (b) copies that do not float, which under the BLKmode size-0 rule requires a shared base symbol. (a) and (b) are contradictory in pure C -- so a premise about the original source is wrong, and the only variable never swept under the PLAIN spelling is the source order of the statements.
frontier.mechanism: sched.c's rank_for_schedule breaks INSN_PRIORITY ties by INSN_LUID -- i.e. by SOURCE ORDER. With almost no memory dependences, nearly every store is priority-tied, so source order directly selects the emitted order. The s1-era note 'order is INERT (11 of 12 identical at 75)' was measured under the ANCHORED spelling only and does NOT transfer to the plain one.
frontier.next_probe: Sweep statement-order permutations from rejected/plain-symbols-wrapper-free-baseline.c (score 61) -- placement of the three loads, the FB0/FB4/FB8 vs FBC/FC0/FC4 vs F80/F84/F88 store groups, and the three copies. Score with tmp/grind/func_8001C624/s2/difffunc.py (reloc-normalised diff), NOT the raw sandbox number.

frontier.hypothesis: The pending-list flush (sched.c:1754, `if (pending_lists_length > 32) flush_pending_lists(insn)`) is an honest, source-level scheduling barrier whose POSITION is determined purely by the COUNT of memory refs preceding it -- and it has never been probed.
frontier.mechanism: flush_pending_lists makes the flushing insn depend on every pending read and write and sets last_pending_memory_flush, which every subsequent memory op then depends on: a total memory fence. Calls also fire it (so the count restarts after each jal). Counting the current body puts the flush inside copy3's third store. Adding, removing, or reordering memory ops upstream MOVES that fence -- a real lever obtainable without any coercion construct.
frontier.next_probe: Instrument the count (or add a BB2_SCHED_DEBUG-style print) to locate the flush insn exactly; then test source orders that place the flush immediately before copy1, so all three copies land after a genuine barrier that GCC itself created.

frontier.hypothesis: If the copies were NOT BLKmode, the size-0 alias pathology disappears entirely and the dependences become exact and honest -- no re-basing, no wrappers.
frontier.mechanism: The size-0 conflict rule only applies to BLKmode MEMs. An element-wise copy emits SImode MEMs whose offsets memrefs_conflict_p compares exactly. The open question is whether any element-wise C spelling still emits the target's per-copy `lui/addiu` base registers and lw/lw/sw/sw pairing (incl. the odd-word load-delay nop in the 12-byte copy) rather than at-form per-element accesses.
frontier.next_probe: Write the three copies element-wise through a local pointer pair and compare the emitted shape against asm/6CAC.s:5064-5117; if the pairing/nop survive, re-derive the dependences and re-measure.

judge_constraints: REGRESSION DIAGNOSIS (completion bar: match must land WITHOUT this construct family): Three distinct cheat constructs found — two unannotated do-while(0) wrappers (one with variant spelling `0 != 0`) and a dead load/store round-trip pair — all permuter-discovered, all framed in the commit by GCC-scheduling intent rather than program spec. Worker must redo the function in pure C, dropping the do-while wrappers and the t/u round-trips; the struct-copy Blk16/Blk12 pattern and the outer sequencing are clean and can stay.  (committed code flagged by the re-audit patrol; review and re-do in pure C if confirmed. The byte-correct construct stays on main until a clean replacement lands.)

Do NOT commit candidate.c's wrapper form. Next session: (1) write the s2 measurements into the DURABLE ledger — hypotheses.md and evidence.md entries and a state.json s2 floor_history entry, with the sandbox numbers, so exhaustion is auditable by someone other than the author; (2) work the three named frontier avenues (sched.c pending-list flush, whole-region struct typing in the header, directed permuter on the anchored base) to a documented dead end. The setVector respell and the `0 != 0` -> `0` normalisation are approved and may be carried forward. If and only if all three avenues are spent and recorded, resubmit a ruling request with the complete ledger — the nested wrappers can then be re-considered under do-while-zero-exception on their merits.
